explanation: OCR (Optical Character Reader) scans printed or typed text and converts the shapes of the characters into machine-readable text, so it images printed text directly. OMR only detects the presence of pencil marks in fixed positions (bubble sheets), and MICR only reads characters printed in magnetic ink (cheque routing numbers), so neither handles general printed text and 'All of above' is wrong.

Answer unverified: the circulated answer key marks B ('dot per sq. inch'), but print resolution is actually specified in DPI = dots per linear inch (300, 600, 1200 dpi), which is option A, and the review panel resolved to A. Option C is the classic trap: dots printed per unit time is printing speed (pages per minute), not output quality, which also rules out 'All of above'. If the grader uses the printed key, expect B; if it uses the real definition, expect A.
